strengths: Current role at re:cinq includes hands-on contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP-based agentic workflow tooling, providing direct relevance to the agentic AI architecture mandate.
Production distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency microservices demonstrates real high-throughput backend depth.
Long track record with event-driven architecture across multiple projects (GISCollective, Dunnhumby, re:cinq) aligns with the event-streaming and real-time inference requirements.
concerns: No executive or formal engineering leadership experience; entire career history carries Senior or Staff individual-contributor titles with no evidence of managing or scaling an engineering organization.
Agentic AI exposure is very recent (April 2025 to present) and described at the tooling/framework level, not as production multi-agent systems operating under financial or regulatory consequences.
No meaningful fintech-infrastructure background — credit risk, working-capital allocation, payment flows, or compliance across jurisdictions are entirely absent from the resume.
response.justification: Bogdan is a technically solid polyglot engineer with genuine distributed-systems depth and nascent exposure to agentic AI tooling, but the CTO role demands demonstrated executive leadership, a track record of scaling and developing engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people, and production-grade multi-agent systems operating in regulated fintech environments — none of which his resume evidences. The seniority gap alone is the dominant disqualifier, and the absence of any fintech-infrastructure or credit-risk domain experience compounds the mismatch against the hard requirements of this role.
